note: Explorer DTD section gets a click-to-expand technical reference panel attached to the section title. After the DTD data has loaded, clicking on '// DTD · DETERMINISTIC TOKEN DISTRIBUTION' toggles a hidden block (default closed) that explains how the Phase 2 lottery actually works: block shape classification (PAYOUT / NORMAL / UPDATE_EMPTY based on lottery_payout), deterministic winner derivation by hashing the 16-block window the consensus rule uses (so anyone can reproduce the selection locally), per-address lifetime eligibility (must have mined at least one block since genesis), the 5-block sliding cooldown that excludes recent miners but NOT past winners, prize composition (drains an accumulating lottery pool, never the miner's 50% share), the 50/25/25 coinbase split is independent of lottery, audit guarantee (the RUN AUDIT tool re-derives every historical winner and flags any divergence), and reward block phases. Title shows a chevron (▾ closed / ▴ open) and is cursor:pointer with a tooltip. Panel uses a purple left-border to match the audit row already on the page. No external API calls. No new endpoint. No consensus / RPC schema / node / miner change. Pure docs UX inside an existing section.
